description: pyad is a Python library to manage Active Directory via ADSI on Windows. Use only on Windows.

documentation.example: from pyad import aduser; u = aduser.ADUser.from_cn('administrator'); print(u)
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/pyad && python3 pyad.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/pyad && python3 pyad.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/pyad && python3 pyad.py --help`. ADSI / Active Directory client (Windows).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web, ad] in the post_exploitation category. Fits when the engagement needs: ADSI / Active Directory client (Windows).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: ADSI / Active Directory client (Windows).

metadata.example: from pyad import aduser; u = aduser.ADUser.from_cn('administrator'); print(u)

name: pyad

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.
